hard.next_step: 6/24/26 Mtg with Katie to confirm Zycus is VOC going into final demo. iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative and Oracle at IT's insistence. IT is a wild card, but no one is happy with Oracle as a partner. VP of SC (Executive Sponsor) wants a vendor-led implementation, something Oracle and iValua won't do. New timeline: final RFP submission with new requirements 7/18/26; greatly minimized focus on direct. Reference request for 3 implementation levels.
hard.ais_why: On the 2/2/2026 demo Yufeng Chen drove the AI conversation himself and pushed on Anna autonomous-negotiation algorithms and guardrails; he committed to share 4,400 lines of Amazon Business transaction data under NDA for a classification-accuracy POC. Classic AI-hungry champion behaviour.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, deeply engaged deal where Zycus is the buyer-declared vendor of choice, but the 24 Jul date will slip: ~9-tab, 35-SME module demos are still unbooked, the final RFP is due 18 Jul, and the economic layer (David Single, Jim Boss) has never joined a call.
north_star_verdict.math: 18 days to close (24 Jul from 6 Jul). Stage Shortlisted since 15 Mar 2026 (~113 days). CloseDate has slipped repeatedly (30 Mar -> 15 May -> 26 Jun -> 24 Jul, the last on 18 Jun). Remaining path: finalize demo agenda, run module demos across ~9 RFP tabs with ~35 SMEs (buyer targeting the 13-17 Jul window, NOT yet booked), final RFP submission 18 Jul, line-by-line SME scoring, finalist decision, IT integration deep-dive, EB/Exec-Sponsor value review, legal/InfoSec, MSA/SOW. Dan Chao (24 Jun): 'originally hoping to close demos this/next week, unrealistic at this point.' Realistic completion is 4-6 weeks, pushing close into late Aug.
evidence: Zycus is vendor of choice going into the final demo (Next Step 24 Jun, Katie Booth). Buyer engagement is intense and current: updated-response call 30 Jun, requirements discussion 24 Jun, requirements review 8 Jun, plus near-daily email through 2 Jul. This is a live, favoured deal, not a stalled one.

The close date is not credible. Module demos across ~9 RFP tabs with ~35 SMEs are not yet scheduled (Dan Chao, 24 Jun: this/next week is 'unrealistic'), final RFP is due 18 Jul, and finalist decision, IT deep-dive, legal and contracting all follow. The 24 Jul date will slip to late Aug.

Economic Buyer David Single (VP Finance) and Executive Sponsor Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) have never appeared on a recorded call. Champions Yufeng Chen and Katie Booth advocate strongly at the manager layer but cannot convert advocacy to budget sign-off without a direct Single/Boss briefing (Avoma attendance; SF Contact Roles).

IT is the live competitive lever: Oracle stays on the shortlist 'at IT's insistence' (Next Step 24 Jun) while the business is openly unhappy with it. CIO Bob King and IT lead Pete gate integration approval, and the IT deep-dive is a formal step before contracting.

No quantified business case exists. The buyer articulated operational pain and asked for pricing ('we do wanna know the dollar in our head, so I can plug that number in leadership's mind', Yufeng Chen 30 Jun) but no ROI, savings or cycle-time figure anchors the $340K for VP Finance.

metrics.narrative: S&C has articulated clear operational pain but quantified none of it. Dan Chao described the manual drawing-package effort ('last week it took me an hour to put a package together, there's 14 drawings', 24 Jun) and the team wants Zycus to aggregate PLM artifacts so sourcing managers do 'more meaningful work'. On 30 Jun Yufeng Chen asked for the price ('we do wanna know the dollar in our head, so I can plug that number in leadership's mind') yet attached no savings, headcount or cycle-time target. Without a quantified model the VP-Finance EB (David Single) has no financial anchor to defend $340K, so building the value case is a ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget owner is David Single (VP Finance) per SF Contact Roles, corroborated by Yufeng Chen's 30 Jun reference to plugging the dollar figure 'in leadership's mind', and Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) is the named Executive Sponsor / Decision Maker. Neither has joined any recorded call, so the EB is mapped but entirely un-engaged: all commercial and pricing conversation has stayed at the manager layer (Yufeng, Katie, Dan). The VP of Supply Chain reportedly wants a vendor-led implementation (Next Step 24 Jun), a power-level preference that favours Zycus but is second-hand. Getting Single/Boss into a value review before the 18 Jul final RFP is the single highest-leverage gap to close.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (David Single VP Finance; Jim Boss VP)

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are explicit and structured: S&C wrote a 217-line, 9-tab RFP/SOW scored line-by-line by module-specific SMEs, with each deliverable drop-down scored 'exactly how it does in the SOW' (Katie Booth 30 Jun). The weighted gaps centre on 3DX/PLM integration (Yufeng: '3DX is likely going to be a must have', 24 Jun) and VMI, while direct-materials eProcurement is being de-weighted. Katie confirmed Zycus won't be adversely scored on de-scoped direct items ('you're not going to be adversely scored... we're very less likely going to use SIM for eProcurement', 24 Jun). Vendor-led implementation and honesty are informal but decisive criteria the buyer has voiced repeatedly.
sources: Yufeng Chen / Ronit Wadhwa call 24 Jun 2026

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal RFP evaluation: gap-clarification sessions (8/24/30 Jun) feed a revised requirements assessment (submitted 22 Jun), then module-by-module demos to ~35 SMEs across ~9 tabs (buyer targeting 13-17 Jul), a final RFP submission on 18 Jul, line-by-line SME scoring, and a finalist decision. Reading the call cast: Dan Chao (program manager) owns scheduling, Yufeng Chen and Katie Booth run the technical/scoring judgement, and IT lead Pete gates the integration deep-dive. Missing from the process so far is any appearance by the finance/exec approvers (David Single, Jim Boss) or the primary DM of record Janki Parekh, so the approval chain above the sourcing team is unproven.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are visible but not yet started. Ronit Wadhwa flagged that custom 3DX/OTM work would be 'a separate line item in the quote... scoped with IT at SOW time when we're contracting' (24 Jun), and referenced working 'an MSA and SOW rather with Rick'. Katie Booth noted S&C runs contracts through DocuSign CLM and wants Zycus to integrate to it rather than replace redlining, which frames a future integration/paper dependency. No legal, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement-onboarding step has begun; the IT integration deep-dive is the next formal gate before paper. Visibility will come once the demo/scoring completes and pricing is submitted.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is operational and clearly owned. S&C's engineering-heavy sourcing forces manual aggregation of drawings/artifacts from multiple systems (Dan Chao: putting a 14-drawing package together by hand, 24 Jun), it lacks supplier-inventory visibility (VMI), and it needs 3DX/PLM drawings pushed to suppliers on sourcing triggers, which Yufeng calls 'so critical for us... as a company growing' (30 Jun). The pain owner is the sourcing function (Yufeng Chen, Dan Chao) with a maturing supply chain. It remains qualitative: real, repeatedly voiced, but never converted into a cost or time figure that finance can weigh.

champion.narrative: Two strong, active champions. Yufeng Chen (Global Sourcing Manager) drives the technical evaluation, repeatedly reassures Zycus it is competitive despite the direct-materials gaps ('you guys are still in a very good shape', 24 Jun; 'none of those will be treated as a deal breaker... it's a line item', 24 Jun) and praises Zycus's candour as partnership behaviour. Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT) confirmed Zycus is vendor of choice going into the final demo and openly downgrades rivals. Dan Chao coordinates SME scheduling and demo logistics. What has developed: technical fit, honesty-built trust, VOC status, de-scoping of Zycus's weakest area. What is missing: neither champion has demonstrated access to David Single or Jim Boss, so advocacy is powerful at the manager layer but cannot yet convert to budget sign-off.

competition.narrative: Zycus is the front-runner on a three-vendor shortlist (vendor of choice, Katie Booth 24 Jun). Oracle is the ERP incumbent kept in 'at IT's insistence' though the business is unhappy with it, and iValua is in 'only because they were shortlisted last initiative' (Next Step 24 Jun) - neither is buyer-preferred. Coupa is dormant, named only in the CRM field and grouped by Ronit with Zycus as an indirect-only player ('the Coupa's and the Zycus of the world will not have direct connections with PLM', 24 Jun). The real exposure is not a rival SRM suite but IT's Oracle lean plus the 3DX/PLM native-connector gap where dedicated direct-sourcing peers (Jaggaer, Ivalua) are stronger - though the buyer has de-scoped direct materials. Zycus wins on vendor-led implementation (Oracle/iValua won't offer it), honesty-built trust, indirect SRM fit, and the Merlin/Anna AI story.

deal_movement.summary: Live, buyer-driven deal accelerating on engagement but not on the calendar. Since the last sweep the buyer submitted a revised requirements assessment (22 Jun), set a firm 18 Jul final-RFP timeline, and Zycus resolved the two open technical gaps (3DX via an app, VMI for indirect and direct). Amount rose 275K to 340K on 26 Jun while direct-materials focus was 'greatly minimized'. The date remains the sole real problem.

items.change: Amount raised from $275K to $340K by Richard Hunsinger as SRM scope firmed
Close date pushed 26 Jun -> 24 Jul (fifth slip in the chain)
Buyer submitted a revised requirements assessment; new final-RFP timeline set for 18 Jul
Zycus confirmed it will build 3DX/PLM capability via an app and that VMI supplier-inventory visibility now exists for indirect and direct (Ronit Wadhwa)
Direct materials / eProcurement focus 'greatly minimized' in scope; Ronit had offered to withdraw if direct stayed in scope
Buyer requested business reference calls across three implementation levels (smooth / some issues / problematic)

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the front-runner and buyer-declared vendor of choice (Katie Booth, 24 Jun) on a three-vendor shortlist. Ranked by CURRENT threat: Oracle is the strongest live threat, kept in 'at IT's insistence' by CIO Bob King and IT lead Pete who gate integration approval, even though the business is openly unhappy with it. iValua is a low, inertia-only threat ('in only because shortlisted last initiative'). Coupa is dormant, named only in the CRM field and grouped with Zycus as an indirect-only player. The deeper exposure is the 3DX/PLM native-connector gap where direct-sourcing specialists (Jaggaer, Ivalua) are stronger, but the buyer has de-scoped direct materials, neutralising it. Win path: close the IT integration deep-dive to remove Oracle's only foothold, lead with vendor-led implementation and Merlin AI, and land the value case with David Single/Jim Boss before finalist scoring.

best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is winning at the manager layer; the win now hinges on reaching the finance/exec approvers, neutralising IT's Oracle lean, and turning trust into a quantified value case before the 18 Jul final RFP scoring.
flags: Multi-thread to the power layer: David Single (VP Finance, EB) and Jim Boss (VP, Exec Sponsor) have never joined a call. Engineer their entry via the champions before 18 Jul scoring.
Neutralise IT's Oracle lean: proactively run the IT integration deep-dive with CIO Bob King and lead Pete, since Oracle survives only 'at IT's insistence'.
Convert VOC and honesty-built trust into a quantified savings/ROI model so a $340K spend has a finance anchor the EB can defend.

champion_strength.summary: Yufeng Chen (Global Sourcing Manager) and Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT) are strong, active champions with multi-call engagement (8, 24, 30 Jun) who confirmed Zycus is vendor of choice, de-risked the 3DX/PLM and VMI gaps as line items rather than deal breakers, and downgrade Oracle and iValua on Zycus's behalf. What has developed: technical fit, honesty-built trust, VOC status and demo momentum. What is missing: neither has shown access to Economic Buyer David Single or Executive Sponsor Jim Boss, so advocacy is powerful at the manager layer but cannot yet convert to budget sign-off. Kara Weiner (VP Supply Chain) is cited as a vendor-led-implementation advocate per Next Step but has not appeared on a recorded call, so her engagement is unconfirmed.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin/Anna is a genuine differentiator and Yufeng drove a strong AI conversation on the 2 Feb demo, but the recent June cycle has been dominated by requirements, integration and 3DX/OTM mechanics, so the agentic story has gone quiet just as scoring approaches. Re-landing Merlin agentic sourcing and Anna negotiation in the module demos is an open opportunity.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate. The field read is driven by the 2 Feb demo where Yufeng Chen personally drove the AI conversation, pushed on Anna autonomous-negotiation guardrails, and committed 4,400 lines of Amazon transaction data under NDA for a classification POC (classic AI-hungry behaviour). Recent June calls did not revisit AI, so the appetite is real but under-fed this cycle.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and warming: buyer-driven cadence across 8/24/30 Jun calls plus near-daily email through 2 Jul, demos being scheduled, and champions actively protecting Zycus. Not cooling.

Champion openly downgrades rivals and confirms VOC (Katie Booth, 24 Jun).

items.expected_effect: Locks the demo window and protects the 18 Jul final-RFP scoring path, arresting further slip.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the buyer-declared vendor of choice on S&C's SRM RFP with two strong sourcing champions (Yufeng Chen, Katie Booth), but the 24 Jul close will slip and the VP-Finance economic buyer (David Single) has never joined a call: the win hinges on landing a quantified value case with the power layer before the 18 Jul final RFP scoring.

win_position.text: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70; the deal earns it on VOC status (Katie Booth named Zycus vendor of choice, Next Step 24 Jun) and two active champions, but holds at ~68 until the VP-Finance economic buyer engages and the date firms.

Yufeng Chen twice reassured Zycus it is 'in a very good shape' and that the 3DX/VMI gaps are 'not a deal breaker, just a line item' (Avoma, 24 Jun), and the buyer de-scoped Zycus's weakest area (direct-materials eProcurement) out of scoring.

No economic buyer has ever joined a call: David Single (VP Finance) and Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) are absent, so a $340K spend has no finance anchor and no quantified ROI (Yufeng: 'we wanna know the dollar', 30 Jun).

Oracle stays on the shortlist 'at IT's insistence' with CIO Bob King gating the integration deep-dive, and the 24 Jul date has already slipped five times with ~9-tab, 35-SME demos still unbooked (Dan Chao: 'unrealistic', 24 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-driven cadence is intense: requirements calls 8/24/30 Jun plus near-daily email, with the customer scheduling module demos for the 13-17 Jul window and a 18 Jul final RFP (Dan Chao, 30 Jun).
Commercial topics have entered: the buyer requested pricing and Amount rose $275K to $340K on 26 Jun as the SRM scope firmed (SF field history).
Demos across ~9 tabs and ~35 SMEs are still unbooked as of 6 Jul, so the 24 Jul close is not credible and will push into late Aug.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested heavily: a 217-line RFP, multiple gap sessions, and a request for three-tier implementation references, signalling genuine intent to buy (Katie Booth, 17 Jun).
The buyer actively protected Zycus by de-scoping direct materials so its eProcurement gap won't be scored against it (Yufeng Chen, 24 Jun).
Commitment stops at the manager layer: no VP/finance sign-off, no exec sponsor on a call, and no mutual close plan yet.

deal_risk.text: The economic layer is unengaged: David Single (VP Finance) has never been on a call, so champions cannot convert advocacy to budget sign-off without a direct briefing.
Oracle persists via IT (CIO Bob King); the integration deep-dive is an unpassed gate before contracting.
Scope narrowed on direct materials (Next Step: 'greatly minimized focus on direct') - a phased, defensive posture that can shrink deal value at contracting despite the recent amount rise.

executive_alignment.evidence: EB David Single and Exec Sponsor Jim Boss never engaged on any recorded call (Avoma attendance).

business_case.evidence: Pain is qualitative only; no ROI/savings figure attached to the $340K (Yufeng Chen, 30 Jun).

timeline.evidence: 24 Jul close will slip: ~9-tab, 35-SME demos unbooked, final RFP due 18 Jul, then scoring/IT/legal (Dan Chao, 24 Jun).

champion_strength.evidence: Two strong active champions who confirmed VOC and downgrade rivals (Katie Booth, Yufeng Chen).

scope_change.from: Full S2C including direct-materials eProcurement (3DX/PLM, VMI, OTM)
scope_change.detail: Buyer 'greatly minimized focus on direct' (Next Step 2 Jul) and Ronit offered to withdraw if direct stayed in scope (Rick, 12 Jun). This narrowing removes Zycus's weakest area and is fit-driven rather than a budget cut: Amount actually rose $275K to $340K on 26 Jun. Watch for a phased-implementation posture that could defer value later.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus is the buyer-declared vendor of choice going into the final demo (Katie Booth, 24 Jun); Yufeng Chen: 'you guys are still in a very good shape' and praised Zycus's candour as partnership behaviour (24 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative operational pain (manual drawing packages, no supplier-inventory visibility, 3DX handling) but no ROI, savings or cycle-time figure; buyer asked for pricing without a value model (Yufeng Chen, 30 Jun).
